info.title: Fax.Plus REST API
info.description:
This is the Fax.Plus API v3 developed for third party developers and organizations. In order to have a better coding experience with this API, let's quickly go through some points:

- This API assumes **/accounts** as an entry point with the base url of **https://restapi.fax.plus/v3**.

- This API treats all date and times sent to it in requests as **UTC**. Also, all dates and times returned in responses are in **UTC**

- Once you have an access_token, you can easily send a request to the resource server with the base url of **https://restapi.fax.plus/v3** to access your permitted resources. As an example to get the user's profile info you would send a request to **https://restapi.fax.plus/v3/accounts/self** when **Authorization** header is set to **Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN** and custom header of **x-fax-clientid** is set to YOUR_CLIENT_ID

get.description: Get account information of all non-admin members of your corporate account. Only the admin account can send a request to this endpoint which returns the accounts of all members (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:member:read**)

mcp.name: get-accounts
mcp.description:
List all corporate members in your account.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getAccounts()`

**Notes:**
- No parameters required
- Only admin accounts can call this endpoint
- Returns account information for all non-admin corporate members

(Requires scopes: personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:member:read, oauth2:all)

get.description: Get information about an account. For members user_id can only be self. For admin it can be either self, or a user_id of any corporate member. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:member:read**, **fax:user:read**)

parameters.description: User ID to get information about. For your own account use **'self'**
parameters.name: user_id

mcp.name: get-user
mcp.description:
Get account information for a user.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Your account: `getUser(user_id='self')`
- Corporate member: `getUser(user_id='<user_id>')`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members

**Notes:**
- Members can only use 'self'
- Admins can query any corporate member

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:member:read, personal_access_token:fax:user:read)

put.description: Modify personal information of your own account or your corporate member's account. user_id can be either self, or a subordinate's user_id. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:member:edit**, **fax:user:edit**)

parameters.description: User ID to modify. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.
parameters.name: user_id

get.description: Get corporate member's role and faxing quota (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:member:read**)

parameters.name: member_user_id
parameters.description: Member user ID

mcp.name: get-member-details
mcp.description:
Get a corporate member's role and faxing quota.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getMemberDetails(member_user_id='<user_id>')`

**Required Parameters:**
- member_user_id: The user ID of the corporate member

**Returns:**
- quota: Monthly page quota for the member
- role: Member's role in the company

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:member:read)

put.description: Update corporate member's role and faxing quota. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:member:edit**)

parameters.name: member_user_id
parameters.description: Member user ID

post.description: Invite new users to join your corporate account by sending them email invitations (maximum 10 per request). Only corporate admins with appropriate permissions can use this endpoint. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:member:edit**)

requestBody.description: List of email addresses to invite

409.description: One or more emails are already registered

description.example: One or more emails are already registered

x-code-samples.source:
import faxplus
from faxplus.rest import ApiException

# Configure API client
configuration = faxplus.Configuration(
    access_token='YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN'
)

with faxplus.ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
    api_client.default_headers['x-fax-clientid'] = 'YOUR_CLIENT_ID'
    api_instance = faxplus.AccountsApi(api_client)
    
    emails = ["user1@example.com", "user2@example.com"]
    
    try:
        response = api_instance.invite_members(emails)
        print("Invitations sent successfully")
    except ApiException as e:
        print(f"Exception: {e}")

curl -X POST 'https://restapi.fax.plus/v3/accounts/self/member-invitations' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN' \
  -H 'x-fax-clientid: YOUR_CLIENT_ID' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '["user1@example.com", "user2@example.com"]'

patch.description: Resend email invitations to users who have not yet signed up for your corporate account (maximum 10 per request). Only corporate admins with appropriate permissions can use this endpoint. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:member:edit**)

requestBody.description: List of email addresses to resend invitations to

x-code-samples.source:
import faxplus
from faxplus.rest import ApiException

# Configure API client
configuration = faxplus.Configuration(
    access_token='YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN'
)

with faxplus.ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
    api_client.default_headers['x-fax-clientid'] = 'YOUR_CLIENT_ID'
    api_instance = faxplus.AccountsApi(api_client)
    
    emails = ["pending1@example.com", "pending2@example.com"]
    
    try:
        response = api_instance.resend_invitations(emails)
        print("Invitations resent successfully")
    except ApiException as e:
        print(f"Exception: {e}")

curl -X PATCH 'https://restapi.fax.plus/v3/accounts/self/member-invitations' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN' \
  -H 'x-fax-clientid: YOUR_CLIENT_ID' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '["pending1@example.com", "pending2@example.com"]'

get.description: List your purchased/assigned phone numbers. For corporate members all assigned numbers will be returned, while for the corporate admin, all purchased numbers. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:numbers:read**)

parameters.description: ID of the user to get numbers for. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.
parameters.name: user_id

mcp.name: list-numbers
mcp.description:
List your purchased or assigned phone numbers.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Your numbers: `listNumbers(user_id='self')`
- Member's numbers: `listNumbers(user_id='<user_id>')`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members

**Notes:**
- Corporate members: Returns all assigned numbers
- Corporate admin: Returns all purchased numbers

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:numbers:read)

get.description: Get information about a single purchased/assigned fax number (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:numbers:read**)

parameters.description: ID of the number owner
parameters.name: user_id

parameters.description: Fax number to get information about
parameters.name: number

mcp.name: get-number
mcp.description:
Get information about a specific fax number.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getNumber(user_id='self', number='+1234567890')`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members
- number: The fax number (e.g., '+1234567890')

**Returns:**
- Number details including status, assignment, acquisition date, and notifications

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:numbers:read)

put.description: Assign fax number to a corporate member (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:numbers:edit**)

parameters.description: ID of the number owner. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID to assign to a corporate member.
parameters.name: user_id

parameters.description: Fax number to update
parameters.name: number

204.description: Assign an already purchased number to your members

delete.description: Revoke fax number from a corporate member. To revoke your own number use self as a user_id. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:numbers:edit**)

parameters.description: ID of the user to revoke the number from. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts. Number cannot be removed from the admin.
parameters.name: user_id

parameters.description: Fax number to remove members from
parameters.name: number

204.description: Revoke number from member that owns fax number

get.description: Get your own or your subordinate's faxes list. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:fax:read**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Category parameter. Valid string values: **inbox** (received faxes), **sent** (outgoing faxes), or **spam** (spam faxes). Optional, defaults to 'inbox'.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Start date to get records from that date. Format: *YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss* (e.g., '2018-01-01 00:00:00'). Optional, if omitted returns all records from the beginning.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: End date to get records before that date. Format: *YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss* (e.g., '2020-01-01 00:00:00'). Optional, if omitted returns all records up to the current time.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of fax records to return per request. Must be between 1 and 50. Optional, defaults to 50. Use with 'offset' for pagination.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset for pagination. Specifies the number of records to skip before starting to return records. Optional, defaults to 0.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort the results by. Valid values: **date**, **from**, **to**, **comment**, **pages**. Optional.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Order of the sorting. Valid values: **asc** for ascending, **desc** for descending. Optional, defaults to 'asc'.

parameters.name: in_progress
parameters.description: Return in-progress incoming faxes. Optional, defaults to false. This functionality can be enabled on request.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by transmission status. Comma-separated list of valid fax statuses

parameters.name: from_number
parameters.description: Filter by source/from fax number in E.164 format

parameters.name: to_number
parameters.description: Filter by destination/to fax number in E.164 format

parameters.name: is_read
parameters.description: Filter by read status. **true** for read faxes, **false** for unread faxes

parameters.name: min_pages
parameters.description: Filter faxes with at least this many pages

parameters.name: max_pages
parameters.description: Filter faxes with at most this many pages

mcp.name: list-faxes
mcp.description:
List fax records for a user. Returns paginated results.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage Examples:**
- List inbox faxes: `listFaxes(user_id='self', category='inbox', after='2024-01-01 00:00:00', before='2024-12-31 23:59:59', limit=50, offset=0, sort_by='date', order='asc', in_progress=false)`
- List sent faxes: `listFaxes(user_id='self', category='sent', after='2024-01-01 00:00:00', before='2024-12-31 23:59:59', limit=50, offset=0, sort_by='date', order='desc', in_progress=false)`
- All faxes (no date filter): `listFaxes(user_id='self', category='inbox', after='2018-01-01 00:00:00', before='2099-12-31 23:59:59', limit=50, offset=0, sort_by='date', order='asc', in_progress=false)`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account
- category: 'inbox' (received), 'sent' (outgoing), or 'spam'
- after: Start date in format YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss (e.g., '2024-01-01 00:00:00')
- before: End date in format YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss (e.g., '2024-12-31 23:59:59')
- limit: Max records per request, 1-50 (use 50 for max)
- offset: Pagination offset (use 0 for first page)
- sort_by: 'date', 'from', 'to', 'comment', or 'pages'
- order: 'asc' or 'desc'
- in_progress: Boolean (use false normally)

**Note:** Due to MCP limitations, all parameters must be provided even though they have defaults in the API.

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:fax:read)

put.description: Bulk update multiple fax records (mark as read/unread and/or update comment). Maximum 100 fax IDs per request. Duplicate IDs are automatically ignored. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:fax:edit**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

delete.description: Bulk delete multiple fax records. Maximum 50 fax IDs per request. Duplicate IDs are automatically ignored. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:fax:edit**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fax IDs to delete (max 50 unique IDs). Duplicates are automatically ignored.

get.description: Get a specific fax record details like duration, pages etc. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:fax:read**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

mcp.name: get-fax
mcp.description:
Get details for a specific fax record.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getFax(user_id='self', fax_id='<fax_id>')`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members
- fax_id: The unique identifier of the fax

**Returns:**
- Fax details including duration, pages, status, cost, timestamps, and more

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:fax:read)

put.description: You can modify a fax record's comment or mark it as read. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:fax:edit**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

delete.description: Delete a specific fax record by providing its id. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:fax:edit**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

204.description: 204 will be return on successful Fax modification

post.description: Before sending a fax you need to upload your files using this API. In order to upload your fax file, you have to send a `multipart/form-data` request with your file. Set the `name` to `fax_file`, `filename` to your file's name with extension, and the Content-Type to the file's MIME type. In most cases, the `filename` directive will be automatically added by your library of choice. If the upload was successful you would receive a `file_path` which you can use to send your fax. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:file:edit**)

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Can be 'pdf' or 'tiff'

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

requestBody.description: A file to be uploaded. The file can be in PDF, TIFF, Word, Excel, RTF or image format.

get.description: Download sent or received fax file. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:file:read**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

parameters.name: fax_id
parameters.description: ID of the fax which you want to download

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: File format for the response. Deprecated - use Accept header instead. Optional.

200.description: Fax file content in corresponding mimetype e.g. application/pdf

mcp.name: get-file
mcp.description:
Download a sent or received fax file.

**IMPORTANT FOR AI AGENTS:** This tool downloads a BINARY FILE (PDF/TIFF). Call this tool to download the file, then notify the user that it's ready for viewing. DO NOT attempt to display or include the binary content in your response. Provide metadata like the fax ID and format.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getFile(user_id='self', fax_id='<fax_id>', format='pdf')`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members
- fax_id: The unique identifier of the fax
- format: 'pdf' or 'tiff' (use 'pdf' for most cases)

**Note:** Due to MCP limitations, all parameters including `format` must be provided.

**Returns:** Binary file content (PDF or TIFF) that will be downloaded to the user's system.

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:file:read)

get.description: Download single page (starting with 1) of a sent or received fax file. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:file:read**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

parameters.name: fax_id
parameters.description: ID of the fax which you want to download

parameters.name: page_num
parameters.description: Number of the page you want to download, starting with 1

mcp.name: get-file-page
mcp.description:
Download a single page from a fax file.

**IMPORTANT FOR AI AGENTS:** This tool downloads a BINARY IMAGE FILE (TIFF). Call this tool to download the page, then notify the user that page [page_num] is ready for viewing. DO NOT attempt to display or include the binary content in your response.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getFilePage(user_id='self', fax_id='<fax_id>', page_num=1)`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members
- fax_id: The unique identifier of the fax
- page_num: Page number to download (starting with 1)

**Returns:** Single page as TIFF image that will be downloaded to the user's system.

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:file:read)

get.description: Retrieve fax confirmation report (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:fax:read**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

parameters.name: fax_id
parameters.description: ID of the fax which you want to download

mcp.name: get-fax-report
mcp.description:
Retrieve a fax confirmation report.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getFaxReport(user_id='self', fax_id='<fax_id>')`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members
- fax_id: The unique identifier of the fax

**Returns:**
- Confirmation report as PDF

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:fax:read)

get.description: Retrieve fax thumbnail (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:fax:read**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

parameters.name: fax_id
parameters.description: ID of the fax which you want to download

mcp.name: get-fax-thumbnail
mcp.description:
Retrieve a fax thumbnail image.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getFaxThumbnail(user_id='self', fax_id='<fax_id>')`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members
- fax_id: The unique identifier of the fax

**Returns:**
- Thumbnail image as JPEG

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:fax:read)

get.description: Get a list of the faxes in the outbox which were not yet sent. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:fax:read**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

mcp.name: list-outbox-faxes
mcp.description:
List faxes in the outbox waiting to be sent.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `listOutboxFaxes(user_id='self')`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members

**Returns:**
- List of outbox fax records that have not yet been sent

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:fax:read)

post.description: Send a fax to one or more destinations. For corporate members without a fax number assigned set the 'from' parameter to 'no_number'. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:fax:edit**). To randomly select one of the available numbers per recipient, use comma-separated string.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

put.description: Bulk update multiple outbox fax records (update comment). Maximum 100 outbox fax IDs per request. Duplicate IDs are automatically ignored. Jobs in 'sending' status cannot be updated and will return a 409 status code. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:fax:edit**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

delete.description: Bulk delete (cancel) multiple outbox fax records. Maximum 10 outbox fax IDs per request. Duplicate IDs are automatically ignored. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:fax:edit**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of outbox fax IDs to delete (max 10 unique IDs, each ID is max 32 characters). Duplicates are automatically ignored.

get.description: Get specific details of a fax currently scheduled for sending. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:fax:read**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.
ID of the outgoing fax to get
parameters.name: outbox_fax_id

mcp.name: get-outbox-fax
mcp.description:
Get details of a fax scheduled for sending.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getOutboxFax(user_id='self', outbox_fax_id='<fax_id>')`

**Required Parameters:**
- user_id: Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a user ID for corporate members
- outbox_fax_id: The unique identifier of the outgoing fax

**Returns:**
- Details of the scheduled fax including status, recipients, files, and settings

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:fax:read)

delete.description: Delete an outgoing fax that is being scheduled for sending. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:fax:edit**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.
ID of the outgoing fax to delete
parameters.name: outbox_fax_id

204.description: 204 will be returned on successful fax deletion from outbox

put.description: Modify an outgoing fax that is being scheduled for sending. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:fax:edit**)

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID. Use 'self' for your own account, or provide a specific user ID for corporate member accounts.
ID of the outgoing fax to update
parameters.name: outbox_fax_id

204.description: 204 will be returned on successful fax modification in outbox

get.description: Returns a list of currently registered webhooks for the requested **event**. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:webhook:read**)

mcp.name: get-webhooks
mcp.description:
Use this to list user webhooks. Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:webhook:read.

Returns a list of currently registered webhooks for the requested **event**. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:webhook:read**)

post.description: Register a new webhook which will be called on a specific event. See the WebhookCallback model. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:webhook:edit**)

delete.description: Delete a webhook by its ID. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:webhook:edit**)

parameters.description: ID of the webhook to delete
parameters.name: hook_id

get.description: Get your account balance. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:shop:read**)

mcp.name: get-balance
mcp.description:
Get your account balance.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `getBalance()`

**Notes:**
- No parameters required

**Returns:**
- Account balance and credit information

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:shop:read)

get.description: Get a list of countries where you can purchase fax numbers. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:shop:read**)

mcp.name: list-countries
mcp.description:
List countries where fax numbers can be purchased.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `listCountries()`

**Notes:**
- No parameters required

**Returns:**
- List of countries with ISO codes, names, and availability status

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:shop:read)

get.description: Get a list of areas where you can purchase fax numbers for a specific country. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:shop:read**)

parameters.name: country_code
parameters.description: Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)

mcp.name: list-areas
mcp.description:
List areas where fax numbers can be purchased for a specific country.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Call: `listAreas(country_code='US')`

**Required Parameters:**
- country_code: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., 'US', 'CA', 'GB')

**Returns:**
- List of areas with codes, names, and setup costs

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:shop:read)

get.description: Get a list of available fax numbers for a specific area. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**, **fax:shop:read**)

parameters.name: country_code
parameters.description: Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)

parameters.name: calling_code
parameters.description: Area code

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Channel to specify whether numbers should be 'random' or 'custom'. Optional, defaults to 'random'.

parameters.name: contains
parameters.description: If 'custom' is selected as the channel, this field allows specifying numbers that should appear in the phone number. Optional, only used when channel is 'custom'.

mcp.name: list-shop-numbers
mcp.description:
Search for available fax numbers in a specific area.

**Authentication:** When using this tool via MCP, authentication is handled automatically by your MCP server configuration. Configure your Personal Access Token (PAT) in your MCP client settings - you don't need to pass authentication parameters manually.

**MCP Tool Usage:**
- Random numbers: `listShopNumbers(country_code='US', calling_code='212', channel='random', contains='')`
- Custom numbers: `listShopNumbers(country_code='US', calling_code='212', channel='custom', contains='1234')`

**Required Parameters:**
- country_code: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., 'US', 'CA')
- calling_code: Area code (e.g., '212', '415')
- channel: 'random' or 'custom' (use 'random' for any available numbers)
- contains: Digits to appear in the phone number (use empty string '' when channel='random')

**Note:** Due to MCP limitations, all parameters must be provided. Use `channel='random'` and `contains=''` for random number search.

**Returns:**
- List of available numbers with pricing information

(Requires scopes: oauth2:all, personal_access_token:fax:all:read, personal_access_token:fax:shop:read)

post.description: Purchase an available fax number. (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**, **fax:shop:edit**)

get.description: Returns metadata about the current access token including type, scopes, expiration date, and PAT-specific information if applicable. For PAT tokens, returns full metadata (id, name, preview, created/expiration dates, last used). For OAuth tokens, returns token type and scopes only.

mcp.name: get-token-info
mcp.description:
Use this to get metadata about the current access token (type, scopes, expiration, etc.). Requires a valid access token (OAuth or PAT), no additional product scopes.

Returns full token information including scopes, expiration date, and PAT-specific metadata if applicable. For OAuth tokens, returns token_type and scopes only.

get.description: Get user contacts with optional search, pagination, or retrieve specific contacts by IDs (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**)

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of contact IDs to retrieve (max 50). When provided, other search/pagination parameters are ignored.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search term to filter contacts by name, fax number, groups, or notes

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit for pagination (max 50)

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Field to sort by

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Sort direction

parameters.name: shared
parameters.description: If true, return shared/corporate contacts. If false, return personal contacts

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Contact ID

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Contact ID

post.description: Share contacts with all corporate members (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**)

get.description: Get contact groups with optional search and pagination. You can also retrieve specific groups by providing comma-separated IDs (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:read**)

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of group IDs to retrieve (max 50). When provided, other filters are ignored

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Search term to filter groups

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit for pagination (max 50)

parameters.name: shared
parameters.description: If true, return shared/corporate groups. If false, return personal groups

post.description: Create a new contact group (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**)

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: Group ID

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: Group ID

post.description: Share groups with all corporate members (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**)

Assign multiple contacts to multiple groups. **Important:** This operation overwrites existing group assignments for the specified contacts - it does not append to them. Contacts will be unassigned from any group that's not included in this list (Permitted scopes: **fax:all:edit**)

groups.description: Array of group IDs to assign to the contacts. This overwrites existing group assignments - contacts will be unassigned from any group that's not included in this list

contact_ids.description: Array of contact IDs to assign to the specified groups

shared.description: If true, operate on shared/corporate contacts and groups. If false, operate on personal contacts and groups

scopes.all: for now when a user grants permission, all grants will be permitted

personal_access_token.description: Personal Access Token (PAT) is a Bearer token used for secure API calls. For direct API calls, the PAT is used in the Authorization header as 'Bearer {PAT}'. For MCP usage, configure your PAT in your MCP client settings (e.g., in your IDE's MCP server configuration) - authentication will be handled automatically.

AccountStatus.description: Your account status which could be active, inactive etc

Binary.description: Binary data. Will save to file and return file path if `_preload_content` is True, otherwise will return binary stream

cost.description: Fax cost in the user currency

from.description: Sender number. Might be a userId for faxes sent or received with free accounts

include_company_logo.description: Whether to include the company logo on the cover page

FaxStatus.description: Fax status. Some failure codes use failed_fs_* values which represent low-level fax transport issues (for example, fax protocol incompatibility, the remote endpoint stopping responding, or remote disconnections). In particular: failed_fs_8/failed_fs_9 indicate protocol incompatibility; failed_fs_31/failed_fs_32 indicate that the remote stopped responding; failed_fs_35/failed_fs_39 indicate that the remote disconnected.

quota.description: Quota of member (number of pages member can send per month)

role.description: Role of member in your company

acquired_date.description: Date and time at which the number was acquired

assigned_to.description: IDs of the user to whom this number is assigned

expiration_date.description: Number expiration date, might be blank

is_canceled.description: True if number is canceled but not yet deleted

NumberStatus.description: Status of your fax number e.g. active, inactive.

Outbox.description: Model for the outbound fax stored in the outbox

ip.description: IP address from which the send request originated

last_updated_status_time.description: Time and date when the send request status was last updated. Format: *YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss*

at.description: Date and time at which status changed. Format: *YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss*

submit_time.description: Date and time when the fax was submitted for sending

enhancement.description: Text enhancement. Set to True to optimize fax file for text.

partially_sent_retry.description: Enable automatic retry for partially sent faxes. Please make sure that the remote will accept chunked submissions before using it

OutboxOptions.description: Additional configuration for sending a fax

OutboxComment.description: Comment to set for the fax job

name_to.description: TO field on the cover page

name_from.description: FROM field on the cover page

subject.description: SUBJECT field on the cover page

message.description: MESSAGE field on the cover page

include_company_logo.description: Use company logo on the cover page

assigned_to.description: User ID of the account to assign number to

PayloadOutbox.description: Model for creating new outbound fax. **Note:** At least one of `files` or `from_fax` must be provided.

from.description: Number to use for sending the fax. Use comma-separated string to randomly select one of the numbers per-recipient

files.description: List of file names to send. Files should be uploaded beforehand. **Required unless `from_fax` is provided.** Can be combined with `from_fax` to append additional files before the referenced fax file.

from_fax.description: Reference to an existing fax whose file to reuse (resend/forward). **Required unless `files` is provided.** Can be combined with `files` to prepend additional documents before the referenced fax file.

fax_id.description: Session ID of the fax to reuse (the 'id' field from fax list/send responses)

user_id.description: User ID who owns the fax. Use 'self' for your own faxes, or a member ID for corporate subordinate faxes. Defaults to 'self'.

send_time.description: Date when to send the fax. Format: **YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss +HHMM**

return_ids.description: Return scheduled fax IDs to use for tracking and with webhooks

PayloadOutboxModification.description: Model for updating the outgoing fax

count.description: Number of tries to send the fax

delay.description: Delay in seconds between two retries

SendFaxResponse.description: Send fax handle response, will contain Destination-to-ID mapping if the corresponding flag was provided

event_time.description: Time of the event. Format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss

target.description: Configured URL target for this webhook

data.description: Callback data, depends on the event type

id.description: Fax session ID. Note that this ID might be different from the one returned by the listFaxes handle, as this ID refers to the faxing session as a whole, with retries included. Both IDs can be used with the API getFile handle

pages.description: Number of pages in the fax

from_number.description: Sender number. Might be a user ID for faxes sent from free accounts

to_number.description: Fax destination number. Might be a user ID for faxes sent from free accounts

start_time.description: Time at which faxing session started. Format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss

scopes.description: List of scopes granted to this token

last_used.description: Last used timestamp in milliseconds (PAT only)

address.example: 123 Main St, Suite 100, New York, NY 10001

is_shared.description: Whether contact is shared with corporate members

total_count.description: Total number of contacts matching the query

notes.description: Additional notes or description for the group

is_shared.description: Whether group is shared with corporate members

total_count.description: Total number of groups matching the query

is_telefax.description: Whether this contact has a human fax operator

shared.description: Whether to share contact with corporate members

is_telefax.description: Whether this contact has a human fax operator

notes.description: Additional notes or description for the group

shared.description: Whether to share group with corporate members

notes.description: Additional notes or description for the group

BulkFaxUpdateRequest.description: Request to bulk update multiple fax records

ids.description: List of fax IDs to update (1-100 unique IDs, each ID is max 32 characters). Duplicates are automatically ignored.

is_read.description: Mark faxes as read (true) or unread (false). Optional, but at least one of is_read or comment must be provided.

comment.description: Comment text to set for all faxes. Send empty string to remove comment. Optional, but at least one of is_read or comment must be provided.

BulkOutboxUpdateRequest.description: Request to bulk update multiple outbox fax records

ids.description: List of outbox fax IDs to update (1-100 unique IDs). Duplicates are automatically ignored.

comment.description: Comment text to set for all outbox faxes. Send empty string to remove comment.

BulkOperationResultItemFax.description: Result for a single fax operation in bulk request

code.description: HTTP-like status code for this item: 204 (success), 404 (not found), 403 (forbidden), 409 (conflict/invalid state), 500 (server error)

BulkOperationResultItemOutbox.description: Result for a single outbox fax operation in bulk request

code.description: HTTP-like status code for this item: 204 (success), 404 (not found), 403 (forbidden), 409 (conflict/invalid state - e.g., job is 'sending'), 500 (server error)

results.description: Per-item results for each fax ID

BulkOperationResultOutbox.description: Bulk operation result for outbox faxes

results.description: Per-item results for each outbox fax ID

PurchaseNumberRequest.description: Request to purchase a fax number
